metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes (V2 TUI queues mid-turn messages)
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

**agent (yes).** kiro-cli's V2 TUI explicitly queues a follow-up message typed while the model is working ‚Äî it is NOT dropped or sent as literal text. The kiro-cli 2.5.1 binary's embedded changelog carries two decisive entries: `"Added UX for managing queued messages in V2 TUI"` and `"Reject queued ! shell-escape commands during processing instead of sending them as literal text"`. The second confirms that messages typed *during processing* are queued (the special-case is only the `!` shell-escape, which is rejected) ‚Äî so a plain follow-up is queued and consumed at the next safe point, exactly like Claude Code. This resolves the question the dispatch flagged as unverified (some TUIs drop or block mid-turn input): kiro V2 queues.

**daemon (full).** The spec asserts three observables: (1) state stays `working` through the queued-message arrival, (2) no `‚Üí ready` flicker when the queued input lands, (3) the cycle still ends at `ready`. irrlicht's kirocli parser drives state off transcript-line shape, not off keystrokes. The queued text is buffered inside the kiro process and is NOT appended to `~/.kiro/sessions/cli/<uuid>.jsonl` until the current turn ends ‚Äî so the daemon's transcript order is `Prompt(A) ‚Ä¶ turn_done, Prompt(B) ‚Ä¶`. Throughout turn A the trailing line is an `AssistantMessage` carrying `toolUse` blocks (parsed `assistant`) or a `ToolResults` line (parsed `tool_result`) ‚Äî neither is `turn_done`, so the session stays `working` with no flicker to `ready`. Only the FINAL text-only `AssistantMessage` of turn A maps to `turn_done` (parser.go lines 71-79, confirmed by parser_test.go's multi-tool sequence ending in a text-only `turn_done`). The queued `Prompt(B)` then drives a fresh `working` span that ends at `ready`. All three observables are produced.

**driver (ready).** The recipe needs `send` (the mid-turn follow-up, with NO preceding `wait_turn` so it lands while turn A is still in flight), `wait_turn`, and `sleep`. All three are in the driver's `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"`. kiro's `send_text` uses `tmux send-keys -l -- "$text"` then `Enter` ‚Äî the same keystroke mechanism the claudecode reference recipe expressed via its `keys` step ‚Äî so a queued mid-turn send is just a `send`; no `keys` primitive is required and there is no driver gap.

## What irrlicht does NOT see (and why that's OK)

The queue itself is invisible ‚Äî there is no event for "user typed text that is now buffered." The spec asserts no such event; it asserts only state invariants on the working span, which the daemon verifies from its event stream. The kiro JSONL also carries NO model/token/cost (those live in the `<uuid>.json` sidecar the v1 parser does not read), so the observation vector is `model: unknown`, `cost_nonzero: false`, `tokens_nonzero: false`.
caveats: CONFIRMED by live recording 2026-06-05 (replaced the PROVISIONAL emission-ordering caveat): the queued Prompt(B) line is appended to the <uuid>.jsonl only AFTER turn A's text-only turn_done ‚Äî never interleaved mid-turn ‚Äî and turn A is one contiguous working span (AssistantMessage(text,toolUse) ‚Üí ToolResults ‚Üí text-only AssistantMessage). daemon=full holds.

RECIPE BACKFLOW (2026-06-05): the original count-to-10 prompt did NOT exercise the mid-turn queue ‚Äî kiro ignores the 'pause between each' instruction and finishes turn A in ~2s, so the follow-up landed after turn A reached ready (recordings 20260605T011455 + 011556 both showed ready-flicker + a waiting transition). The recipe now forces a BLOCKING shell tool (sleep 8) under --trust-all-tools for a genuine long working span, and a plain-word follow-up ('acknowledged') so turn B ends in ready not waiting.

Mid-turn send is a single `send` with NO preceding `wait_turn`: the second prompt must land while turn A is still working. The driver's send_text bumps EXPECTED_TURNS, so the trailing wait_turn expects both turns (A then queued B) before declaring ready.

kiro may either fold the queued message into the current turn or consume it as a new turn after turn A's boundary; the spec accommodates both ‚Äî the phase contract does not assume one shape. (Observed: consumed as a new turn.)
No model/token/cost in the kiro JSONL (sidecar-only, unread in v1) ‚Äî observations assert model unknown, cost/tokens zero; not a daemon downgrade for this lifecycle-only scenario.

sources.note: Embedded changelog: 'Added UX for managing queued messages in V2 TUI' and 'Reject queued ! shell-escape commands during processing instead of sending them as literal text' ‚Äî confirms the V2 TUI queues mid-turn messages (only the ! shell-escape is rejected), so agent_supports=yes.

Lines 40-83: Prompt‚Üíuser_message; AssistantMessage with toolUse‚Üíassistant (keeps working); text-only AssistantMessage‚Üíturn_done; ToolResults‚Üítool_result. State is transcript-shape driven ‚Äî a contiguous working span with no premature turn_done.

Lines 148-161: the multi-tool turn sequence (Prompt‚ÜíAM(toolUse)‚ÜíToolResults‚ÜíAM(toolUse)‚ÜíToolResults‚ÜíAM(text-only)) asserts EventType progression ending in a single turn_done ‚Äî confirms the working span stays open across tool cycles.

¬ß4-5: JSONL appends turn-by-turn; turn-end is the final text-only AssistantMessage; no model/usage/cost in the JSONL (sidecar only). Grounds the daemon emission model and the empty observation vector.
DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash wait_turn sleep"; send_text uses tmux send-keys -l + Enter ‚Äî the recipe's send/wait_turn/sleep are all elicited, no keys gap, driver_capability=ready.

Canonical scenario: state stays working through the queued-message arrival, no ‚Üíready flicker, cycle ends at ready; the irrlicht primitive is that the transcript does NOT emit a turn boundary when the queued message arrives.

preconditions: kiro-cli 2.5.1 on PATH and authenticated (subscription auth; KIRO_API_KEY not required)
irrlichd is recording (--record / grant-all) and tailing ~/.kiro/sessions/cli/
Launched as `kiro-cli chat --trust-all-tools` so the blocking shell tool runs without a per-tool approval picker
setup: Driver launches the TUI under tmux, waits for the idle input line, and discovers the fresh <uuid>.jsonl as the newest session file after the launch MARKER

The first prompt must keep kiro WORKING long enough to type a follow-up into the TUI. A count-to-10 does NOT suffice ‚Äî kiro ignores the 'pause between each' instruction and flushes all 10 lines in one ~2s turn (live recording 20260605T011455/011556), so the follow-up lands AFTER turn A reached ready, never mid-turn. The reliable long working span is a BLOCKING shell tool call (sleep 8) under --trust-all-tools: kiro stays working across the toolUse/ToolResults cycle (pane shows '‚óè Shell sleep 8 ‚Ä¶ esc to cancel').

The mid-turn follow-up is a single `send` (tmux send-keys -l + Enter) with NO preceding wait_turn, so it lands while turn A is still working; kiro's V2 TUI queues it until the next safe point. The follow-up text must elicit a PLAIN-WORD reply (not a question) so turn B ends in ready, not waiting ‚Äî kiro's 'what's your follow-up?' reply otherwise trips the question/cue ‚Üí waiting heuristic.

script.text: Run this shell command using your shell tool: sleep 8 && echo finished. After it completes, reply with exactly the word: done
Reply with exactly the word: acknowledged

verify: events.jsonl shows the UUID entering ready, then a ready‚Üíworking transition for the first prompt

events.jsonl shows turn A as a contiguous working span across the toolUse/ToolResults cycle (the blocking shell tool); the queued follow-up's Prompt line is appended only AFTER turn A's text-only turn_done
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting at any point (turn B's terminal reply is a plain word, not a question)
events.jsonl eventually shows a working‚Üíready transition; final session state is ready

transcript.jsonl: Prompt(A) ‚Üí AssistantMessage(text,toolUse) ‚Üí ToolResults ‚Üí AssistantMessage(text)=turn_done, THEN Prompt(B) ‚Üí AssistantMessage(text); the queued follow-up appears as a second Prompt consumed after turn A's turn_done
